2603.12288.title: From Garbage to Gold: A Data-Architectural Theory of Predictive Robustness
2603.12288.paper_summary: Tabular machine learning presents a paradox: modern models achieve state-of-the-art performance using high-dimensional (high-D), collinear, error-prone data, defying the "Garbage In, Garbage Out" mantra. To help resolve this, we synthesize principles from Information Theory, Latent Factor Models, and Psychometrics, clarifying that predictive robustness arises not solely from data cleanliness, but from the synergy between data architecture and model capacity. Partitioning predictor-space "noise" into "Predictor Error" and "Structural Uncertainty" (informational deficits from stochastic generative mappings), we prove that leveraging high-D sets of error-prone predictors asymptotically overcomes both types of noise, whereas cleaning a low-D set is fundamentally bounded by Structural Uncertainty. We demonstrate why "Informative Collinearity" (dependencies from shared latent causes) enhances reliability and convergence efficiency, and explain why increased dimensionality reduces the latent inference burden, enabling feasibility with finite samples. To address practical constraints, we propose "Proactive Data-Centric AI" to identify predictors that enable robustness efficiently. We also derive boundaries for Systematic Error Regimes and show why models that absorb "rogue" dependencies can mitigate assumption violations. Linking latent architecture to Benign Overfitting, we offer a first step towards a unified view of robustness to Outcome Error and predictor-space noise, while also delineating when traditional DCAI's focus on label cleaning remains powerful. By redefining data quality from item-level perfection to portfolio-level architecture, we provide a theoretical rationale for "Local Factories" -- learning from live, uncurated enterprise "data swamps" -- supporting a deployment paradigm shift from "Model Transfer" to "Methodology Transfer'' to overcome static generalizability limitations.
2603.12288.paper_summary_zh: 表格式機器學習呈現出一個悖論：現代模型使用高維（high-D）、共線性、易出錯的數據達到了最先進的性能，這違背了「垃圾進，垃圾出」的格言。為了解決這個問題，我們綜合了信息理論、潛在因子模型和心理測量學的原則，澄清了預測的穩健性不僅僅來自數據的清潔，而是來自數據架構與模型能力之間的協同作用。將預測變數空間的「噪聲」劃分為「預測器誤差」和「結構不確定性」（來自隨機生成映射的信息缺失），我們證明了利用高維的易出錯預測變數集在漸進上克服了這兩種類型的噪聲，而清理低維集則根本受到結構不確定性的限制。我們展示了為何「信息共線性」（來自共享潛在原因的依賴性）增強了可靠性和收斂效率，並解釋了為何增加維度減少了潛在推斷的負擔，使得在有限樣本下變得可行。為了解決實際限制，我們提出了「主動數據中心AI」，以高效識別能夠增強穩健性的預測變數。我們還推導了系統性誤差範疇的邊界，並展示了為何吸收「流氓」依賴的模型可以減輕假設違規。我們將潛在架構與良性過擬合聯繫起來，提供了朝向對結果誤差和預測變數空間噪聲的穩健性統一觀點的第一步，同時也劃定了傳統DCAI專注於標籤清理仍然強大的情況。通過將數據質量從項目級的完美重新定義為投資組合級的架構，我們為「本地工廠」提供了理論依據——從實時、未經策劃的企業「數據沼澤」中學習——支持從「模型轉移」到「方法論轉移」的部署範式轉變，以克服靜態可概括性的限制。
2603.12288.author: Terrence J. Lee-St. John et.al.
2603.12288.authors: Terrence J. Lee-St. John, Jordan L. Lawson, Bartlomiej Piechowski-Jozwiak
